attachment.content:
CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman when:
- Security warnings
- Irreversible action confirmations
- Multi-step sequences where fragment order or omitted conjunctions risk misread
- Compression itself creates technical ambiguity (e.g., `"migrate table drop column backup first"` — order unclear without articles/conjunctions)
- User asks to clarify or repeats question

Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

STATUSLINE SETUP NEEDED: The caveman plugin includes a statusline badge showing active mode (e.g. [CAVEMAN], [CAVEMAN:ULTRA]). It is not configured yet. To enable, add this to /Users/vansh/.claude/settings.json: "statusLine": { "type": "command", "command": "bash \"/Users/vansh/.claude/plugins/cache/caveman/caveman/ef6050c5e184/hooks/caveman-statusline.sh\"" } Proactively offer to set this up for the user on first interaction.

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **lite** | No filler/hedging. Keep articles + full sentences. Professional but tight |

| **wenyan-ultra** | Extreme abbreviation while keeping classical Chinese feel. Maximum compression, ultra terse |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"

- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

- wenyan-full: "物出新參照，致重繪。useMemo .Wrap之。"
- wenyan-ultra: "新參照→重繪。useMemo Wrap。"

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- lite: "Connection pooling reuses open connections instead of creating new ones per request. Avoids repeated handshake overhead."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."
- ultra: "Pool = reuse DB conn. Skip handshake → fast under load."

attachment.content:
- remotion-best-practices: Best practices for Remotion - Video creation in React
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman-stats: Show real token usage and estimated savings for the current session. Reads directly from the Claude Code session log — no AI estimation. Triggers on /caveman-stats. Output is injected by the mode-tracker hook; the model itself does not compute the numbers.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:cavecrew: Decision guide for delegating to caveman-style subagents. Tells the main thread WHEN to spawn `cavecrew-investigator` (locate code), `cavecrew-builder` (1-2 file edit), or `cavecrew-reviewer` (diff review) instead of doing the work inline or using vanilla `Explore`. Subagent output is caveman-compressed so the tool-result injected back into main context is ~60% smaller — main context lasts longer across long sessions. Trigger: "delegate to subagent", "use cavecrew", "spawn investigator/builder/reviewer", "save context", "compressed agent output".
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress FILEPATH or "compress memory file"
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

see i want you to create a small personal booksmarks web-app think like linktree and pocket is integrated, i will describe the idea in 5 points
1. people can sign up and login with email + password. new sign ups will recieve a welcome/confiramtion mail
2. a signed-in user can add,edit and delete their own bookmarks. each bookmark will have a title, a url and a pub/pvt flag
3. We will have RBAC a users bookmarks can only be seen by him this is a real requirement of the idea, assume people will poke at your urls and api direclty
4. each user will have public profile with its unique handle anyone without loging in can visit the <handle> and see that users public bookmarks only. and handle will be unique two people cant have same handle so in db the type will be TEXT UNIQUE.
5. once logged in a user should have a simple dashboard of their bookmarks. logged out vistors shouldnt be able to reach it. 
for db we will use supabase for auth also same only, for sending email we will use resend. we will use next.js and it will be a monorepo so the components also can be usable okay. dont code right now first explain me what u understood so i can verify our both vision matches. dont assuem anything ask any questions where u get confused.

content: CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE (full). Drop articles/filler/pleasantries/hedging. Fragments OK. Code/commits/security: write normal.

content.text:
Got it. Summary of understanding:

**Auth**: Supabase Auth, email+password. Resend sends welcome/confirm email on signup.

**Bookmarks**: title + URL + pub/pvt flag. Owner-only CRUD.

**RBAC**: Supabase RLS enforced server-side. Not just UI hiding — API + DB both locked. Direct URL/API pokes get nothing.

**Public profile**: `/<handle>` route, no login needed, shows only public bookmarks of that user. Handle = TEXT UNIQUE in DB.

**Dashboard**: `/dashboard` or similar, gate with session. Shows all bookmarks (pub+pvt). Logged-out → redirect to login.

**Stack**: Next.js monorepo, Supabase (DB+Auth), Resend (email), reusable component packages.

---

Questions before touching code:

1. **Handle setup**: User picks handle during signup flow? Or after, on separate onboarding/settings screen? Can they change it later?

2. **Email confirmation**: Supabase has built-in confirm-link flow. Want to use that (user clicks link to activate) or skip verification and just send welcome email via Resend?

3. **Monorepo shape**: `apps/web` + `packages/ui` (shared components)? Any other apps planned — API, mobile, anything?

4. **URL enrichment**: Auto-fetch OG title/favicon when user pastes URL? Or pure manual entry?

5. **Dashboard**: Just list view? Need search, filter by pub/pvt, tags, pagination?

6. **Styling**: Tailwind? shadcn/ui? Other?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content:
1. handle setup should be during sign-up flow only
2. no skip the verification just send a welcome email via Resend
3. right now forget about shared components jsut focus on building then later we will discuss about shared componenets
4. auto-fetch og title/favicon when user pastes url
5. seach is needed, filter by pub/pvt and should be sorted by dates added and pagination is needed when the user has more than 5 books go to a new page then
6. shad cn ui. i will give u the code for that. clear now?? tell me what u need?

content: CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE (full). Drop articles/filler/pleasantries/hedging. Fragments OK. Code/commits/security: write normal.

content.text:
Clear. Final understanding locked:

- Signup: email + password + handle (unique, picked at registration)
- Email: Resend sends welcome mail, no verification gate
- Single Next.js app for now, revisit monorepo later
- OG fetch (title + favicon) auto on URL paste
- Dashboard: search + pub/pvt filter + sort by date added + pagination (5/page)
- shadcn/ui

---

Need from you to start:

1. **Supabase project URL** + **anon key** + **service role key**
2. **Resend API key**
3. **shadcn/ui code** you mentioned

Also — Next.js version preference? App Router (14/15) or Pages Router?

content.text:
Clean. Now the `.env.local` needs your secrets. Here's everything:

---

**Fill in `.env.local`:**
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=        ← Project Settings → API → Project URL
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=   ← Project Settings → API → anon key
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=       ← Project Settings → API → service_role key
RESEND_API_KEY=                  ← Resend dashboard → API Keys
NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=http://localhost:3000
```

**Then run this SQL in Supabase SQL editor:** `supabase-schema.sql` (at repo root)

**One Supabase setting to disable** — go to Auth → Settings → "Enable email confirmations" → turn OFF (we handle email manually via Resend)

**Start dev:**
```bash
npm run dev
```

**Test order:**
1. `localhost:3000/signup` → create account → check dashboard redirects
2. Add bookmark with URL → OG title auto-fetches on blur
3. Toggle pub/pvt, test search + pagination
4. `localhost:3000/<your-handle>` → public profile (logged out)
5. Try hitting `/dashboard` logged out → should redirect to `/login`

content: Built a full-stack bookmarks app with Next.js 15, Supabase auth and RLS, Resend email, and OG fetch. Fill in .env.local with your Supabase and Resend keys, run the SQL schema in Supabase, then npm run dev. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text: RLS issue — at signup time, `auth.uid()` not set yet in server action context, so profile insert fails the `auth.uid() = id` policy. Fix: use service role client for profile insert.

content.text:
Tables don't exist yet. Schema SQL was never run in Supabase.

Go to **Supabase dashboard → SQL Editor → New query**, paste the contents of `supabase-schema.sql`, run it.

content.content:
1	-- Run this in your Supabase SQL editor
2	
3	-- Profiles table (extends auth.users)
4	create table if not exists profiles (
5	  id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade primary key,
6	  handle text unique not null,
7	  email text not null,
8	  created_at timestamptz default now()
9	);
10	
11	-- Bookmarks table
12	create table if not exists bookmarks (
13	  id uuid default gen_random_uuid() primary key,
14	  user_id uuid references auth.users(id) on delete cascade not null,
15	  title text not null,
16	  url text not null,
17	  favicon_url text,
18	  is_public boolean default false not null,
19	  created_at timestamptz default now(),
20	  updated_at timestamptz default now()
21	);
22	
23	-- Indexes
24	create index if not exists bookmarks_user_id_idx on bookmarks(user_id);
25	create index if not exists bookmarks_created_at_idx on bookmarks(created_at desc);
26	create index if not exists profiles_handle_idx on profiles(handle);
27	
28	-- RLS
29	alter table profiles enable row level security;
30	alter table bookmarks enable row level security;
31	
32	-- Profiles policies
33	-- Anyone can look up a profile by handle (needed for public profile page)
34	create policy "profiles_public_select" on profiles
35	  for select using (true);
36	
37	-- Users can only update their own profile
38	create policy "profiles_owner_update" on profiles
39	  for update using (auth.uid() = id);
40	
41	-- Only the trigger/service role inserts profiles (via signup action)
42	-- We use service role in the signup server action, so this policy is for anon/authed fallback
43	create policy "profiles_owner_insert" on profiles
44	  for insert with check (auth.uid() = id);
45	
46	-- Bookmarks policies
47	-- Owners see all their own bookmarks
48	create policy "bookmarks_owner_select" on bookmarks
49	  for select using (auth.uid() = user_id);
50	
51	-- Anyone sees public bookmarks (for public profile pages)
52	create policy "bookmarks_public_select" on bookmarks
53	  for select using (is_public = true);
54	
55	-- Owners insert their own
56	create policy "bookmarks_owner_insert" on bookmarks
57	  for insert with check (auth.uid() = user_id);
58	
59	-- Owners update their own
60	create policy "bookmarks_owner_update" on bookmarks
61	  for update using (auth.uid() = user_id);
62	
63	-- Owners delete their own
64	create policy "bookmarks_owner_delete" on bookmarks
65	  for delete using (auth.uid() = user_id);
66

content.text:
Also — signup created your auth user but profile insert likely also failed (same missing table). After running SQL, also insert your profile manually:

```sql
-- Run after the schema SQL, replace values with yours
insert into profiles (id, handle, email)
values (
  '<your-auth-user-id>',  -- Supabase Auth → Users → copy UUID
  'vansh',
  'vanshsoniofficial@gmail.com'
);
```

Or just sign out → delete the auth user → sign up again fresh after tables exist.
